mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST rise together, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with negative 5-day forward returns during expansion phases, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening labor demand, which raises household income expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no consistent directional relationship with 20-day forward returns, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.01 over five years.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative forward returns or a Rank IC below 0.02 during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch that forces corporate inventory drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative returns, with forward returns remaining positive in over 55 percent of triggered cases.
